name: Port of Vénasque
description:
This hike takes you to the gates of Spain on one of the most impressive border crossings: the port of Vénasque. A real fault nestled between two rock walls, this gateway offers a fabulous panorama of the largest peak in the Pyrenees: Aneto.

Concretely, the route is divided into two distinct parts: the first which is carried out exclusively uphill to the crossing point, then the descent via the same path.
The climb begins immediately at the exit of the Hospice de France, located in a remarkable green setting. This section includes more than 1,000 meters of elevation gain. As you progress, the environment evolves. The green softness gives way to mineral and rocky terrain. The Vénasque refuge and lakes allow you to take a break before the final part of the climb.

Nestled between two rock walls, the port of Vénasque is a narrow passage at an altitude of 2,444 meters. In front of you stands Aneto, king of the Pyrenees from its height of 3,404 meters, while behind you the view overlooking the Pique valley is just as extraordinary. Savor these breathtaking views before returning to the starting point on the same path.
tips: At the end of the Pique Valley is the Hospice de France. Dating back to the 17th century, this inn has always been intended to receive travelers who venture here. It is located in a superb green setting not far from the Franco-Spanish border.

The Vénasque lakes, also nicknamed the “Booms of Vénasque”, are located at an altitude of 2,250 meters, not far from the refuge of the same name. They offer a magnificent panorama overlooking the valley.

The port of Vénasque, or “puerto de Benasque” in Spanish, is a pass in the Pyrenees located on the Franco-Spanish border. Culminating at 2,444 meters above sea level, it separates the Pique valley to the north and the Esera valley to the south.

Its small rocky passage nestles between the Mine and the Pic de Sauvegarde. The panorama is absolutely magnificent, both on the French and Spanish sides.

waypoints.name: Port de Vénasque / Puerto de Benasque
